Strategy

Investment Strategy

ShoreVest underwrites to recoverable value rather than headline yield. Across all sub-strategies, every deal is anchored by hard collateral, enforceable legal control, and multiple exits so capital stays resilient through volatile cycles.

At a Glance

  • Core focusChina Special Situations
  • Execution modelOriginate to Recovery
  • Primary collateralHard-Asset Backed

The Opportunity

China is the world’s second-largest credit market, but specialist alternative credit remains less crowded than developed peers. That supply-demand mismatch creates strong entry points for managers with local legal, servicing, and restructuring execution.

Market Snapshot
Scale creates repeatable volume; selectivity drives quality.

Sourcing and Pipeline

Two decades of relationships with banks, AMCs, law firms, and servicers support bilateral flow, better pricing, and higher certainty of close.

Wide sourcing universe allows selective deployment.

How We Invest

Every position follows the same operating rhythm: secure collateral, structure control, monitor actively, and realize through the best available exit channel.

OriginateDirect channels with borrowers and institutions.
StructureFirst-lien / enforceable documents and covenants.
MonitorContinuous legal, collateral, and cash-flow oversight.
RecoverWorkout, enforcement, and disciplined disposition.

Sub-Strategies and Dynamic Allocation

Capital allocation flexes by cycle. Use the scenario toggles to view how portfolio balance shifts as market conditions change.

Asset-Based Lending32%
Asset Restructuring53%
Debt Resolutions15%
Illustrative portfolio mix for strategy communication only.
75%Target collateral coverage

Legal Environment and Recovery Paths

PRC enforcement is materially more functional than many offshore allocators assume, with improved auction transparency and clearer creditor pathways for properly structured claims.

Tier-1 courts now process secured claims with greater timeline predictability.

Risk Management Framework

Downside management is structural—not discretionary. The portfolio is built to preserve capital first and monetize upside second.

Underwriting Controls

  • Conservative entry LTV/CTV bands with stress-tested recovery assumptions.
  • Priority claims, enforceability review, and PRC-law documentation standards.
  • No dependence on refinancing to complete value realization.

Portfolio Controls

  • Diversification by borrower, collateral, vintage, and province.
  • Continuous surveillance from origination through disposition.
  • Multiple pre-defined exits reduce duration and execution risk.

Returns Profile

The strategy targets high-teens net returns in supportive environments, with deal-level gross IRRs often exceeding 20%, while maintaining shorter weighted-average duration than many private-market alternatives.
